page: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core metrics placing in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Memory subsystem performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25 percent, while uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain average at 77.59 GB/sec and 44.66 GB/sec respectively. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in image processing and text generation for small language models. However, large-scale workloads reveal clear hardware limitations; prompt processing for a 70B parameter model registers in the bottom 25 percent, and text generation for the same model is unsupported due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC32ads_cc_v5 offers a high-density memory profile and substantial bundled local storage without the premium cost associated with specialized GPU hardware. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on CPU-based execution for vector and matrix operations, which limits its utility for heavy deep learning training or large-scale LLM inference. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, database hosting, medium-scale web serving, software compilation, and data processing tasks that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio and fast local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1229 GB local disk, supporting Premium Storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics sit in the average tier. Lacking GPU acceleration, it exhibits weak performance on large-scale LLM inference tasks, but excels in small-model text generation and image processing. This configuration represents a balanced, cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring substantial local storage and memory capacity without the overhead of specialized accelerators.
og_description: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure EC32ads_cc_v5 is an x86_64 VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual machine featuring high memory density, bundled local storage, and strong single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and a 1229 GB local disk
Delivers strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Lacks GPU acceleration, resulting in limited large-scale LLM inference capabilities
Suited for database hosting, memory-intensive applications, and software compilation
